summary:
An array cost gives the price of stepping off each stair, where cost[i] is paid when you leave stair i. From any stair you may climb either one or two stairs forward.

You can begin at index 0 or index 1, and your goal is to move past the final stair. Return the smallest total cost needed to reach the top.

examples.explanation: Start at index 1, pay 15, and take two steps to climb past the end.
Start at index 0 and skip the costly index-1 and index-5 stairs, paying 1 six times.

constraints: You may start from index 0 or index 1
The top is reached by stepping beyond the last index
